authoritative_sources.title: Certified Tester Foundation Level Syllabus v4.0.1
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISTQB publishes recognized public testing syllabi and glossary-aligned terminology for test activities, work products, reviews, defect management, and test process boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: quality intake, test analysis, test design, test execution evidence, defect triage, and review closure
practical_use_in_pack: scenario design for missing support and defect triage
boundary between quality recommendations and final approval
used_for: evaluation scenarios qes-01 through qes-06 and qes-13
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when ISTQB publishes a new CTFL syllabus or glossary update.

authoritative_sources.title: TMMi Model
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: TMMi Foundation publishes a recognized test process improvement model for managed, defined, measured, and optimized testing practices.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: test policy, planning, monitoring, control, defect prevention, and process improvement
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when the TMMi Foundation updates the model or process-area guidance.

authoritative_sources.title: ISO/IEC 25010 Systems and software quality models
authoritative_sources.publisher: International Organization for Standardization / International Electrotechnical Commission
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO/IEC 25010 is an international quality model reference for software product and system quality characteristics used to structure quality criteria.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: quality attribute selection, acceptance criteria review, control objective framing, and quality risk classification
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review annually or on ISO/IEC revision, amendment, or replacement publication.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-218 Secure Software Development Framework Version 1.1
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public security and software quality guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SSDF is official U.S. federal secure software development guidance covering verification, vulnerability response, evidence discipline, and secure release practices.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: security-aware quality planning, verification evidence review, defect remediation, and vulnerability handoff
practical_use_in_pack: boundary between quality support and security risk acceptance
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review annually or when NIST updates SSDF, federal secure software guidance, or related SP 800 series guidance.

authoritative_sources.title: OWASP Application Security Verification Standard
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OWASP ASVS is a recognized public verification standard for application security controls that quality plans often test without becoming security risk acceptance.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: security control verification, negative-case planning, quality checklist design, and security escalation
practical_use_in_pack: authz, validation, logging, and data protection test prompts
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when OWASP ASVS publishes a new release or material verification-level change.

authoritative_sources.title: GitHub Actions Workflows
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub official documentation defines workflow, job, trigger, and repository evidence behavior for a common CI quality-gate system.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: CI evidence retrieval, quality gate interpretation, workflow run references, and pipeline handoff
practical_use_in_pack: CI run and workflow evidence expectations
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitHub changes workflow syntax, runner behavior, artifacts, or security recommendations.

authoritative_sources.title: Reviewing changes in pull requests
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub official documentation defines PR review comments, approvals, requested changes, dependencies, and feedback incorporation in a common software delivery record.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: review finding status, remediation tracking, reviewer dependency, approval boundary, and resubmission evidence
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitHub changes pull request review, required review, or approval behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: Configuring retention for GitHub Actions artifacts and logs
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub official documentation defines retention options for workflow artifacts and logs, which are common quality evidence workpapers.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: evidence retention, audit-trail packet design, client delivery, and retention-conflict escalation
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitHub changes artifact and log retention behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: OpenAPI Specification
authoritative_sources.source_class: Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The OpenAPI Initiative publishes the authoritative public specification for HTTP API contract documents, operations, schemas, security schemes, and reusable components.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: API contract artifact review, compatibility assessment, schema-driven test design, and evidence requirements
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when the OpenAPI Initiative publishes a new minor or major version.

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 9110: HTTP Semantics
authoritative_sources.source_class: Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF RFC 9110 defines normative HTTP method, status, representation, and semantic behavior for API quality assertions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: HTTP API behavior assertions, protocol edge cases, status-code checks, idempotency, and retry quality expectations
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval, IETF errata, or successor RFC publication.

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 2119: Key words for use in RFCs to Indicate Requirement Levels
authoritative_sources.source_class: Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: RFC 2119 standardizes requirement language used to interpret MUST, SHOULD, MAY, and prohibition terms in acceptance criteria and contracts.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: acceptance criteria interpretation, quality gate wording, and ambiguity escalation
practical_use_in_pack: conflicting or ambiguous policy scenario qes-16
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval, IETF errata, or replacement by updated BCP guidance.

authoritative_sources.title: Node.js Test Runner
authoritative_sources.source_class: Official language, framework, runtime, and platform documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Node.js publishes official runtime test runner documentation for JavaScript projects using built-in test execution.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: stack-specific test execution evidence and runtime-specific test behavior when tenant projects use Node.js
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when Node.js updates active LTS test runner behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: unittest - Unit testing framework
authoritative_sources.source_class: Official language, framework, runtime, and platform documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Python Software Foundation publishes official unittest documentation for Python test discovery, fixtures, assertions, and outcomes.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: stack-specific Python test execution evidence and unit test behavior when tenant projects use unittest
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when Python documentation changes for supported versions.

authoritative_sources.title: How to invoke pytest
authoritative_sources.source_class: Official language, framework, runtime, and platform documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: pytest publishes official documentation for test discovery, invocation, selection, and execution behavior used in Python quality evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: pytest execution evidence, test selection, flaky test reproduction, and report interpretation
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when pytest releases documentation or behavior changes.

authoritative_sources.title: ACM Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ACM is a major professional computing society and its ethics code covers quality, harm avoidance, privacy, confidentiality, competence, and duty boundaries for computing professionals.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: confidentiality, professional judgment, client-delivery evidence handling, harm escalation, and refusal to exceed authority
practical_use_in_pack: authority boundary scenarios qes-10 and qes-15
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review annually or on ACM code revision.

workflow_stages.name: Quality intake and scope classification
workflow_stages.description: Confirm request intent, software artifact, delivery context, tenant facts, authority sources, confidentiality terms, approval matrix, retention rules, and output shape before deciding whether the request is in scope, blocked, refused, or escalated.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Authoritative evidence retrieval
workflow_stages.description: Retrieve and reconcile requirements, acceptance criteria, source diffs, API contracts, schemas, test inventories, CI runs, logs, artifacts, coverage, defects, incidents, release gates, review notes, client commitments, and approval records before issuing a quality status.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Official language, framework, runtime, and platform documentation
Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation

workflow_stages.name: Control objective and inspection application
workflow_stages.description: Apply documented quality standards, software quality attributes, acceptance criteria, API/protocol expectations, security-adjacent verification controls, and tenant release gates to available evidence.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability
Public security and software quality guidance

workflow_stages.name: Defect, incident, and nonconformance handling
workflow_stages.description: Classify defects, repeated failures, incidents, flaky tests, missing RCA, incomplete retest evidence, nonconformances, and corrective-action gaps while preserving incident command, security, legal, and release approval boundaries.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation

workflow_stages.name: Disposition and hold or release recommendation
workflow_stages.description: Prepare evidence-backed pass, fail, hold, release-recommended, investigation-needed, nonconforming, or escalation-required recommendations without claiming final signoff or bypass authority.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Client delivery, confidentiality, and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Package sanitized quality evidence, record assumptions, handle retention and recipient authorization, and escalate when client rights, professional judgment, legal/privacy, security risk acceptance, contractual signoff, or release authority determines the answer.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

artifacts: Change request, user story, acceptance criteria, design document, architecture note, API contract, schema, event contract, dependency matrix, and release note
Pull request diff, review comment, requested changes record, reviewer approval, branch protection status, and resubmission evidence
Test inventory, unit/integration/API/contract/regression/UI smoke tests, exploratory charter, coverage report, CI workflow run, test report, log, trace, screenshot, and artifact link
Defect, incident, bug history, flaky-test register, RCA, corrective-action record, nonconformance, remediation owner, retest proof, and unresolved-risk register
Quality plan, test matrix, quality gate recommendation, hold or release recommendation, release readiness summary, review memo, and quality or safety review note
Client engagement scope, confidentiality terms, retention settings, approved recipient list, approval matrix, client commitment, document repository reference, and delivery packet
systems_of_record: Source repository and pull request review system
Requirements, backlog, design document, API contract, schema registry, and acceptance criteria systems
CI/CD workflow system, test report store, coverage dashboard, artifact store, and release checklist
Test management system, defect tracker, incident management system, RCA tracker, corrective-action tracker, quality risk register, and flaky-test register
Observability, logging, tracing, monitoring, synthetic check, and customer-impact evidence platforms
Document management, engagement scope, client commitment, confidentiality, approval, retention, and policy records

allowed: Classify quality engineering requests as in-scope, blocked, hold, release-recommended, investigation-needed, nonconforming, escalated, or refused.
Recommend quality strategy, test coverage, review findings, defect triage, corrective action, and evidence requirements from named records.
Prepare quality plans, review memos, test matrices, quality gate recommendations, release readiness recommendations, incident summaries, and corrective-action escalations.
State assumptions, uncertainty, missing evidence, source freshness, and approval dependencies.
Route work to adjacent specialists or human reviewers when ownership shifts.
escalate: Required facts, evidence, systems of record, authority sources, approval records, retention rules, or confidentiality terms are missing, stale, or contradictory.
Public source guidance and tenant policy conflict without documented precedence.
Client rights, professional judgment, legal/privacy interpretation, security risk acceptance, release approval, contractual signoff, or regulated duty determines the answer.
A repeat defect, incident, nonconformance, or corrective action lacks RCA, owner, retest evidence, or closure support.
A client-delivery evidence packet has unresolved recipient, retention, redaction, confidentiality, or approval conflict.
refuse: Bypass hold, release, safety, change, incident, review, confidentiality, retention, approval, or quality-control boundaries without explicit authority.
Approve releases, clear holds, close findings, sign contracts, publish client evidence, accept legal/privacy risk, accept security risk, or provide final attestation.

Own infrastructure build-out, environment provisioning, incident command, platform operations, runner operations, cloud architecture, network operations, procurement, product strategy, GTM strategy, or executive roadmap planning.
Disclose tenant, client, source, test, defect, vulnerability, incident, credential, production, contractual, or quality evidence records to unapproved recipients or channels.

domain_failure_modes: Generic quality advice that lacks traceable requirements, code, test, CI, defect, release, or policy evidence.
Clearing a hold, release gate, finding, nonconformance, or corrective action without sufficient workpaper support or authorized reviewer evidence.
Treating a quality gate recommendation as final release approval or contractual signoff.
Overreaching into platform operations, incident response, CI/CD execution, product strategy, cloud architecture, security risk acceptance, privacy legal interpretation, or procurement.
Missing repeat-defect signals because incidents, reopened bugs, flaky tests, RCA, and retest proof are not reconciled.
Using stale or stack-inappropriate public guidance when tenant runtime, framework, CI, contract, or policy context materially changes the answer.
Publishing client or tenant evidence without approved recipient, retention, redaction, confidentiality, and engagement-scope support.
Confidently synthesizing conflicting public source, tenant policy, client commitment, or approval records instead of escalating.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes.
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist.
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing.
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis.
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions.
Client or content workflow handling with evidence and approval awareness.
Escalation when confidentiality, professional judgment, or contractual authority changes the action.

unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The public baseline includes Node.js, Python unittest, and pytest examples, but tenant runtime and framework versions must be retrieved before stack-specific guidance is final.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant policy precedence over public baseline
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The spec requires escalation when public guidance and tenant policy conflict without documented precedence; no blocking ambiguity remains for the validated public spec.

notes: Research contract version 2026-04-08 is satisfied for the professional-and-information-services domain pack.
Required source classes are covered and each source records authority rationale, workflow usage, refresh interval, decay policy, and review date.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated spec-pack use.
